member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage
member.summary: Current stage in the token deployment lifecycle.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Initialising
member.summary: Deployment is being initialised; inputs are being validated.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Validating
member.summary: Input validation is running; guardrails are being evaluated.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Submitting
member.summary: Deployment transaction has been submitted to the network.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Confirming
member.summary: Waiting for on-chain confirmation of the deployment transaction.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Completed
member.summary: Deployment completed successfully; asset is live on-chain.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Failed
member.summary: Deployment failed; see error details for remediation guidance.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentStage.Cancelled
member.summary: Deployment was cancelled by the caller before submission.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.IdempotencyStatus
member.summary: Idempotency state of a deployment request.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.IdempotencyStatus.New
member.summary: First-time request; no prior record found.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.IdempotencyStatus.Duplicate
member.summary: Duplicate request; an identical prior request exists and is being returned.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.IdempotencyStatus.Conflict
member.summary: Conflicting request; idempotency key reused with different parameters.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.IdempotencyStatus.InProgress
member.summary: Prior request is still in progress; this call is attached to the in-flight operation.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType
member.summary: Category of a telemetry lifecycle event.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.StageTransition
member.summary: A deployment stage transition occurred.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.ValidationError
member.summary: A validation error was detected.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.GuardrailTriggered
member.summary: A guardrail check produced a warning or failure.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.IdempotencyHit
member.summary: An idempotency cache hit was detected.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.RetryAttempt
member.summary: A retry was attempted for a previously failed deployment.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.DependencyFailure
member.summary: A dependency (IPFS, blockchain node) responded with a transient error.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.CompletionSuccess
member.summary: Deployment completed successfully.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TelemetryEventType.TerminalFailure
member.summary: Deployment terminated with a terminal failure.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.GuardrailSeverity
member.summary: Severity level of a reliability guardrail finding.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.GuardrailSeverity.Info
member.summary: Informational note; no action required.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.GuardrailSeverity.Warning
member.summary: Warning: potential issue detected; deployment can still proceed.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.GuardrailSeverity.Error
member.summary: Error: invariant violated; deployment must not proceed.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentOutcome
member.summary: Overall outcome category of a deployment attempt.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentOutcome.Success
member.summary: Deployment succeeded; asset is live.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentOutcome.PartialSuccess
member.summary: Deployment partially completed; some steps succeeded.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentOutcome.TransientFailure
member.summary: Deployment failed due to a transient error; retry is advised.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentOutcome.TerminalFailure
member.summary: Deployment failed due to a terminal error; retry will not help without remediation.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentOutcome.Unknown
member.summary: Outcome is not yet known; deployment is still in progress.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentExecutionMode
member.summary: Controls whether the deployment lifecycle service requires authoritative blockchain evidence or accepts deterministic simulation evidence.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentExecutionMode.Authoritative
member.summary: The service requires authoritative blockchain evidence. If the evidence provider cannot supply confirmed on-chain data (e.g. no live node available), the deployment transitions to with and error code BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE. This mode is required for production-grade sign-off and enterprise regulated issuance.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.DeploymentExecutionMode.Simulation
member.summary: The service accepts deterministic simulation evidence when authoritative data is unavailable. Blockchain fields (AssetId, TransactionId, ConfirmedRound) are derived from the deployment ID hash. is set to true on all simulated completions. This mode is acceptable for contract-shape validation, development, and test environments.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus
member.summary: Granular status of deployment evidence availability. Used by downstream consumers (frontend, compliance dashboards, audit tools) to determine the trustworthiness of reported blockchain values.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus.Confirmed
member.summary: Evidence is confirmed authoritative on-chain data retrieved from a live blockchain node or indexer. AssetId, TransactionId, and ConfirmedRound can be relied upon as production-grade proof.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus.Pending
member.summary: Evidence is pending; the blockchain indexer or node has not yet reported the transaction. This is a transient state during indexer lag. Callers should poll the status endpoint and retry after a short delay.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus.Simulated
member.summary: Evidence is deterministically simulated from the deployment ID hash. Not blockchain-confirmed. Suitable for development and contract-shape testing only.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus.Stale
member.summary: Evidence is stale; it was retrieved previously but the indexer has since reported a newer state (e.g. the asset was destroyed after creation). Callers should re-query for current status.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus.Unavailable
member.summary: Evidence is unavailable. The provider could not be reached or returned no data. In mode this causes a terminal failure with error code BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleEvidenceStatus.Invalid
member.summary: Evidence was returned by the provider but could not be validated or parsed. Indicates a malformed response from the upstream blockchain node or indexer.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory
member.summary: Structured error category that classifies the root cause of a deployment failure. Used together with error codes and remediation hints so downstream consumers can decide whether to retry, escalate, or remediate.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.ValidationFailure
member.summary: Input validation failed; the request contains invalid or missing fields.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.EvidenceUnavailable
member.summary: Authoritative blockchain evidence could not be obtained. The provider returned null or threw a non-transient exception.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.NetworkTimeout
member.summary: The evidence provider request timed out. This is a transient failure; the deployment may be retried after a suitable delay.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.MalformedProviderResponse
member.summary: The evidence provider returned a response that could not be parsed or validated. Indicates a malformed upstream response.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.UnsupportedNetwork
member.summary: The target network is not supported by the configured evidence provider. Requires operator configuration changes before retry.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.GuardrailViolation
member.summary: A reliability guardrail check blocked the deployment from proceeding. The guardrail findings in the response contain remediation guidance.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.ProviderException
member.summary: The evidence provider threw an unexpected exception that is not classified as a timeout or malformed-response error. Requires investigation.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.IdempotencyConflict
member.summary: An idempotency key was reused with different deployment parameters (conflict).

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorCategory.InternalError
member.summary: An unexpected internal error occurred. Not categorised into a more specific class.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest
member.summary: Request to initiate or resume a deterministic token deployment lifecycle. All fields participate in idempotency key generation; identical inputs always produce the same lifecycle result.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.IdempotencyKey
member.summary: Idempotency key supplied by the caller. If omitted, a key is derived from the deployment parameters. Resubmitting the same key returns the cached result without re-deploying.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.CorrelationId
member.summary: Caller-supplied correlation ID for distributed tracing.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.TokenStandard
member.summary: Token standard to deploy (e.g., "ASA", "ARC3", "ARC200", "ERC20"). Required.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.TokenName
member.summary: Token name (1–64 characters).

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.TokenSymbol
member.summary: Token symbol / unit name (1–8 characters).

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.Network
member.summary: Target network identifier (e.g., "algorand-mainnet", "base-mainnet").

member.summary: Whether to enable clawback authority on the token.

member.summary: Maximum number of retry attempts before the deployment is considered terminal.

member.summary: Timeout for the entire deployment pipeline in seconds.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest.ExecutionMode
member.summary:
Controls whether the deployment requires authoritative blockchain evidence or accepts deterministic simulation evidence. Defaults to .
In mode the service fails with BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE if confirmed on-chain data cannot be obtained, making it suitable for production regulated issuance sign-off.
In mode the service uses deterministic hash-derived values and sets = true; suitable for development, contract-shape testing, and non-production environments.

member.summary: Human-readable remediation hint when Outcome is a failure category.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleResponse.IsSimulatedEvidence
member.summary:
Indicates that the blockchain-side evidence fields (, , ) are derived deterministically from the deployment ID rather than obtained from a live blockchain node. When true, these values are deterministic placeholders suitable for contract-shape validation and sign-off workflow testing, but they do NOT represent confirmed on-chain state.
A value of false means the evidence was obtained from a real blockchain confirmation and can be used as authoritative proof of deployment.
Sign-off environments and frontend consumers MUST check this flag before treating or as production-valid evidence.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleResponse.EvidenceProvenance
member.summary:
Human-readable description of where the deployment evidence originated. Provides traceability for operators, audit tools, and downstream dashboards.
Representative values:
"algorand-indexer" — evidence retrieved from a live Algorand indexer node; authoritative on-chain state.
"simulation" — evidence derived deterministically from the deployment ID hash; not blockchain-confirmed.
"unavailable" — no evidence could be obtained; set on failure responses when authoritative mode is requested.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleResponse.EvidenceAvailability
member.summary:
Structured evidence availability detail. Provides fine-grained status of the deployment evidence so downstream consumers can distinguish pending, confirmed, simulated, stale, unavailable, and invalid states without parsing and heuristically.
This field is always populated on responses from the lifecycle service.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleResponse.ErrorDetail
member.summary: Structured error detail when is a failure category. Null on successful responses.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleResponse.ProviderContext
member.summary: Context describing the evidence provider that was used for this deployment. Supports multi-chain extensibility: consumers can determine which provider (Algorand, EVM, Simulation, etc.) produced the evidence without inspecting provider-specific fields.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail
member.summary: Structured description of deployment evidence availability. Enables downstream consumers (frontend, compliance dashboards, audit tools) to reliably distinguish pending, confirmed, simulated, stale, unavailable, and invalid evidence without heuristic string parsing.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail.Status
member.summary: Machine-readable evidence status. See for semantics.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail.ReasonCode
member.summary: Machine-readable reason code for the current status. Examples: CONFIRMED_AUTHORITATIVE, SIMULATED_HASH_DERIVED, PROVIDER_NULL_RETURN, PROVIDER_TIMEOUT, PROVIDER_EXCEPTION, MALFORMED_RESPONSE.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail.Message
member.summary: Human-readable explanation of the evidence status. Suitable for audit logs and operator dashboards.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail.IsRetriable
member.summary: Whether the evidence failure is transient and the deployment may be retried. false means the failure is terminal and requires corrective action. Only meaningful when is or .

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail.RetrievedAt
member.summary: Timestamp when the evidence was retrieved from the provider (UTC). Null if evidence could not be obtained.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.EvidenceAvailabilityDetail.DiagnosticDetail
member.summary: Sanitised diagnostic detail from the provider. Empty on success. Useful for operational debugging without exposing raw exception messages.

member.summary: Machine-readable error code (e.g. BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE).

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleErrorDetail.ErrorCategory

member.summary: Whether the failure is transient and the deployment may be retried after a delay. false means the error requires corrective action before retry.

member.summary: Human-readable remediation guidance for operators and enterprise issuers.

member.summary: Sanitised diagnostic context (key-value pairs) for operational debugging. Values are sanitised to prevent log injection.

member.summary: Context about the evidence provider used for a deployment. Enables provider-neutral lifecycle contracts so future EVM or other network evidence providers plug into the same contract without breaking fail-closed semantics.

member.summary: Whether the provider is operating in a simulation (non-blockchain) mode. Mirrors for provider-oriented consumers.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.LifecycleProviderContext.IsAvailable
member.summary: Whether the provider is configured and reachable. false means no live provider is available; failures will be terminal in mode.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence
member.summary:
On-chain evidence returned by a IDeploymentEvidenceProvider for a completed deployment transaction.
When is true these values are deterministic hash-derived placeholders (suitable for contract-shape validation only). When is false these values were obtained from a live blockchain node and represent confirmed on-chain state.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence.TransactionId
member.summary: On-chain transaction identifier. For Algorand this is the base32-encoded tx ID; for EVM chains this is the 0x-prefixed transaction hash.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence.AssetId
P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence.ConfirmedRound
P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence.IsSimulated
member.summary: When true, these values are deterministic simulations derived from a hash of the deployment ID and do NOT represent confirmed blockchain state. Sign-off tooling MUST treat IsSimulated = true as non-authoritative evidence.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence.ObtainedAt
member.summary: ISO-8601 timestamp when the evidence was obtained or generated.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.BlockchainDeploymentEvidence.EvidenceSource
member.summary:
Identifies the data source that produced this evidence. Machine-readable label for audit trails and downstream consumers.
Representative values:
"algorand-indexer" — obtained from a live Algorand indexer API call; represents confirmed on-chain state.
"simulation" — derived deterministically from the deployment ID hash; not blockchain-confirmed. will also be true.

member.remarks: This model provides a canonical schema for token metadata that can be used by wallet integrations, frontends, and other consumers. It includes all essential fields needed for proper token display and user experience, with validation and fallback support.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType
member.summary: Represents the various types of tokens supported by the system.
member.remarks: This enumeration categorizes tokens based on their characteristics, such as quantity, decimals, and metadata standards. It includes support for both Algorand Standard Assets (ASA) and Ethereum-based ERC20 tokens, with further distinctions for fungibility and metadata.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType.ASA_NFT
member.summary: ASA where quantity is equal to 1 and decimals is equal to 0

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType.ASA_FNFT
member.summary: ASA where sum of quantity is equal to 1, but decimals are not 0

member.summary: ASA_NFT with ARC3 metadata pointing to json file with metadata

member.summary: ASA_FNFT with ARC3 metadata pointing to json file with metadata

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.VerificationJourneyApprovalReady
member.summary: A live-provider verification journey reached the ApprovalReady stage — all checks passed with provider-backed evidence.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.VerificationJourneyDegraded
member.summary: A live-provider verification journey entered a degraded state due to a provider error, missing configuration, or missing provider dependency. No approval should be issued. Actionable diagnostics are available.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.VerificationJourneyFailed
member.summary: A live-provider verification journey entered a terminal failure state. Evidence cannot be used for approval gating; a new journey must be started.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.ProtectedSignOffApprovalWebhookReceived
member.summary: An approval or escalation webhook was received and persisted for a protected sign-off flow. Emitted for all non-malformed outcomes (Approved, Escalated, Denied).

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.ProtectedSignOffEscalationWebhookReceived
member.summary: An escalation webhook was received and persisted. The case has been escalated to a higher reviewer; a final approval or denial is still pending.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersisted
member.summary: A protected sign-off evidence pack was successfully persisted for a head ref. Includes freshness status and release-grade flag.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.ProtectedSignOffReleaseReadySignaled
member.summary: A release-readiness evaluation determined that the current head is fully ready for promotion: all required approvals received and evidence is complete and fresh.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.ProtectedSignOffEvidenceStale
member.summary: A release-readiness evaluation determined that evidence is stale or was captured against a different head ref. Re-execution of the sign-off workflow is required.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Webhook.WebhookEventType.ProtectedSignOffHeadMismatch
member.summary: A protected sign-off evidence evaluation detected a head mismatch — the evidence pack head ref does not match the evaluated head ref.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Models.Whitelist.WhitelistRule
member.summary: Represents a whitelisting rule for RWA tokens aligned with MICA requirements
member.remarks: Whitelisting rules define automated conditions and policies for managing participant access to RWA tokens. Rules enable compliance automation, policy enforcement, and audit trails required by MICA (Markets in Crypto-Assets) regulation.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Program
member.summary: Configures and runs the web application for the Biatec Tokens API.
member.remarks: This class sets up the necessary services, middleware, and configurations for the API, including controllers, Swagger/OpenAPI documentation, authentication, and token services. It is the entry point of the application and is responsible for building and starting the web host.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Program.Main(System.String[])
member.summary: Configures and runs the web application.
member.remarks: This method sets up the web application by configuring services, middleware, and endpoints. It initializes Swagger for API documentation, configures authentication using Algorand, and registers various services and repositories required for the application. The method then builds and runs the application.
member.param: The command-line arguments used to configure the application.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Repositories.AmlRepository
member.summary: In-memory repository implementation for AML screening records

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Repositories.ApprovalWorkflowRepository
member.summary:
In-memory singleton repository for approval workflow stage decisions and audit events.
Registered as a singleton so that decisions and audit events survive individual HTTP requests and DI scope recycling within a single application process.
For persistent-across-restart storage, replace this implementation with a database-backed version while keeping the same interface contract.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Repositories.ApprovalWorkflowRepository.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Repositories.ApprovalWorkflowRepository})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Repositories.ComplianceCaseRepository
member.summary:
In-memory singleton repository for compliance case persistence.
Registered as a singleton so that cases, idempotency keys, and export logs survive individual HTTP requests and DI scope recycling within a single application process.
For restart-durable storage, replace this implementation with a database-backed version (e.g., Entity Framework Core, Dapper) while keeping the same interface contract.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Repositories.IssuerWorkflowRepository
member.summary:
In-memory singleton repository for issuer workflow team members and workflow items.
This implementation provides durable-within-process storage: data survives individual HTTP requests and DI scope changes because the repository is registered as a singleton. It serves as the authoritative state store for the .
For persistent-across-restart storage, replace this implementation with a database-backed version while keeping the same interface contract.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.AlgorandDeploymentEvidenceProvider
member.summary:
Retrieves authoritative blockchain deployment evidence from an Algorand indexer node.
This provider queries the Algorand indexer REST API to search for an asset-configuration transaction (tx-type=acfg) whose note field begins with the deployment ID (UTF-8 bytes, base64-encoded). This requires the actual token deployment transaction to embed the deployment ID in its note — which is the established contract between the Algorand token services and the evidence layer.
When evidence is found and the transaction has been confirmed on-chain, is set to false and is set to "algorand-indexer".
Fail-closed semantics: the provider returns null — never simulated data — in all of the following scenarios:
The indexer node is unreachable ().
The request times out (controlled by ).
The indexer returns a non-HTTP-2xx status code.
The indexer response cannot be parsed as valid JSON.
The response contains no matching confirmed transaction.
The network is not an Algorand-family network (e.g., EVM chains).
The is not configured.
In mode, a null return from this provider causes the lifecycle service to transition the deployment to with error code BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE.
Supported Algorand-family networks: algorand-mainnet, algorand-testnet, algorand-betanet, voi-mainnet, aramid-mainnet.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.AlgorandDeploymentEvidenceProvider.#ctor(System.Net.Http.IHttpClientFactory,Microsoft.Extensions.Options.IOptions{BiatecTokensApi.Configuration.DeploymentEvidenceConfig},Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Services.AlgorandDeploymentEvidenceProvider})
member.summary: Initialises the provider with the configured HTTP client factory and options.

member.summary: Internal constructor for unit testing — accepts a pre-built config directly, avoiding IOptions.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Services.AlgorandDeploymentEvidenceProvider.IsSimulated
member.remarks: Always false — this provider retrieves confirmed on-chain state, not deterministic simulations.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ApprovalWorkflowService
member.summary:
Repository-backed implementation of the enterprise approval workflow service.
Provides deterministic posture calculation, stage decision recording, evidence readiness evaluation synthesised from stage states, and tamper-evident audit history for multi-stage enterprise release pipelines.
Posture derivation is deterministic and testable: 1. Any Rejected stage → BlockedByStageDecision 2. Any release-blocking Missing evidence → BlockedByMissingEvidence 3. Any release-blocking Stale evidence → BlockedByStaleEvidence 4. Any release-blocking ConfigurationBlocked evidence → ConfigurationBlocked 5. All 5 stages Approved → LaunchReady 6. Otherwise → BlockedByStageDecision (stages still pending/blocked)

member.summary: Synthesises evidence readiness items from the current stage records. Each stage maps to one evidence item; freshness is derived from stage status and age.

member.summary: Derives the active owner domain: the domain responsible for the next action. Evaluated in stage order; the first non-approved stage owns resolution. NeedsFollowUp overrides to Requestor; ConfigurationBlocked to Platform.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ARC1400TokenService
member.summary: Provides functionality for deploying and interacting with ERC-20 token contracts on blockchain networks.
member.remarks: The class is designed to facilitate the deployment of ERC-20 token contracts and manage interactions with the BiatecToken smart contract. It loads the ABI and bytecode for the BiatecToken contract from a JSON file and uses this information to deploy contracts and perform related operations. This service relies on blockchain configuration settings and application-specific settings provided via dependency injection. It also logs relevant information and errors during operations. Ensure that the required ABI and bytecode file ("BiatecToken.json") is present in the "ABI" directory under the application's base directory.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, loading the ABI and bytecode for the BiatecToken contract and configuring the service.
member.remarks: This constructor reads the ABI and bytecode for the BiatecToken contract from a JSON file located in the "ABI" directory under the application's base directory. The loaded ABI and bytecode are used to interact with the BiatecToken smart contract. Ensure that the "BiatecToken.json" file is present and correctly formatted in the expected location.
member.param: The configuration monitor for blockchain-related settings.
The configuration monitor for application-specific settings.
The logger used to log information and errors for this service.
HTTP context accessor for correlation ID extraction
member.exception: Thrown if the BiatecToken contract bytecode is not found in the ABI JSON file.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ARC1400TokenService.ValidateRequest(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ARC1400.Request.ARC1400TokenDeploymentRequest,BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType)
member.summary: Validates the deployment request for an ARC1400 token based on the specified token type.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ARC1400TokenService.CreateARC1400TokenAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ARC1400.Request.ARC1400TokenDeploymentRequest,BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType)
member.summary: Deploys an ERC-20 token contract to the specified blockchain network.
member.remarks: This method deploys an ERC-20 token contract using the provided deployment parameters. The initial supply is allocated to the specified receiver address, or to the deployer's address if no receiver is provided. The method handles exceptions and logs relevant information about the deployment process.
member.param: The deployment request containing the token details, such as name, symbol, decimals, initial supply, and the blockchain configuration (e.g., chain ID and RPC URL).
member.returns: A containing the deployment result, including the contract address, transaction hash, and success status. If the deployment fails, the response includes an error message.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ARC3TokenService
member.summary: Service for creating and managing ARC3 Fungible Tokens on Algorand blockchain
Initializes a new instance of the class, configuring it to interact with Algorand nodes and IPFS repositories based on the provided options.
member.remarks: During initialization, the service attempts to connect to each allowed Algorand network specified in the configuration. For each network, it validates the connection by retrieving transaction parameters and logs the connection status. If a connection to any network fails, an exception is thrown, and the service cannot be initialized.
member.param: A monitor for that provides configuration settings, including allowed networks and authentication details for connecting to Algorand nodes.
An instance used for logging information, warnings, and errors related to the service's operations.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Arc76CredentialDerivationService
member.summary:
Implements deterministic ARC76 credential-based Algorand account derivation.
Algorithm: - Email is canonicalized (lowercase + trim) before derivation - Uses which applies PBKDF2-based derivation per the ARC-0076 specification - Slot 0 is always used (one account per credential set) - Returns a deterministic Algorand Ed25519 account — same email+password always produces the same address
Security: - Private key material is never logged - The 25-word Algorand mnemonic (from ToMnemonic()) encodes the private key and must be encrypted before storage - Public key is safe to return to callers
Test Vector (documented in docs/arc76.md): email = "testuser@biatec.io", password = "TestPassword123!", slot = 0 → address = "4DV7T4TUCD4KCPMLCD2GHQGKNX4PTZPMTNJLH77DEH7ZPZHAIAYG5JBBRI"

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Arc76CredentialDerivationService.DerivationSlot
member.summary: Slot number used for ARC76 account derivation. Slot 0 is the primary account.

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Services.AuthenticationService.DerivationContractVersion
member.summary: Version of the ARC76 derivation contract implemented by this service. Increment when derivation rules change to allow clients to detect breaking changes.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.AuthenticationService.CanonicalizeEmail(System.String)
member.summary: Canonicalizes email address for deterministic ARC76 account derivation
member.param: Raw email address from user input
member.returns: Canonicalized email (lowercase, trimmed)
member.remarks:
Email canonicalization ensures: - Deterministic ARC76 account derivation (same email -> same account) - Prevents duplicate accounts from case/whitespace variations - Consistent lookup behavior across registration/login
Canonicalization rules: 1. Trim leading/trailing whitespace 2. Convert to lowercase (email addresses are case-insensitive per RFC 5321)
Business Value: Eliminates support incidents from users unable to login due to case mismatch between registration and login.
Risk Mitigation: Prevents authorization drift and account access issues that would undermine enterprise governance requirements.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.BackendDeploymentLifecycleContractService
member.summary:
Implements the deterministic backend deployment lifecycle contract with ARC76 hardening.
Key guarantees: 1. Determinism – same email+password always derives the same ARC76 Algorand address. 2. Idempotency – repeated requests with identical idempotency keys return cached results. 3. State machine – illegal lifecycle transitions are blocked with explicit error codes. 4. Error taxonomy – all failures are classified into a stable machine-readable taxonomy. 5. Audit telemetry – every significant event is recorded with correlation IDs.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ComplianceAuditExportService
member.summary: In-memory implementation of .
member.remarks:
Assembles scenario-specific compliance audit export packages by composing KYC/AML decisions, onboarding case records, compliance blockers, sign-off evidence, and approval-workflow history into durable, provenance-backed packages.
Readiness semantics are fail-closed (priority order):
Provider unreachable → DegradedProviderUnavailable
Unresolved critical blocker → Blocked
Missing required evidence → Incomplete
Required evidence stale → Stale
Approval review pending → RequiresReview
All checks pass → Ready
In a production implementation the deterministic in-memory seed behaviour would be replaced by calls to KycAmlDecisionIngestionService, ComplianceCaseManagementService, ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService, and ApprovalWorkflowService.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ComplianceOrchestrationHardeningService
member.summary: Hardening service for enterprise compliance orchestration.
member.remarks: Aggregates jurisdiction constraint evaluation, sanctions/KYC readiness, and launch gate enforcement with deterministic, auditable outcomes. All providers that are not yet fully integrated return explicit "not configured" states rather than silently passing — ensuring no unsafe silent success patterns. Error responses carry typed values so callers can distinguish invalid input, provider outages, policy failures, and internal exceptions.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ComplianceOrchestrationService
member.summary: Orchestrates KYC and AML compliance checks, maintains an in-memory auditable decision trail, and enforces idempotency for repeated requests with the same key.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ComplianceOrchestrationService.DeriveIssuancePosture(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ComplianceOrchestration.NormalizedComplianceDecision)
member.summary: Derives the issuance posture — whether token launch is hard-blocked, advisory-flagged, or clear — based on the current compliance decision state and any detected sanctions. This is a pure, deterministic function: the same decision always produces the same posture.
member.param: The normalised compliance decision to evaluate.
member.returns: An describing whether launch should be blocked and why.
member.remarks: Priority order (highest to lowest): 1. Rejected with SANCTIONS_MATCH reason code → (hard block) 2. NeedsReview with REVIEW_REQUIRED reason code (potential AML hit) → (hard block) 3. Rejected (any other reason) → (hard block) 4. NeedsReview → (hard block) 5. Expired → (hard block) 6. InsufficientData → (hard block) 7. Pending → (advisory — not yet decided) 8. ProviderUnavailable → (advisory) 9. Error → (advisory) 10. Approved → (clear)

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ComplianceOrchestrationService.DeriveWatchlistCategories(System.String)
member.summary: Derives human-readable watchlist category labels from well-known AML reason codes. Returns an empty list for non-sanctions reason codes.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ComplyAdvantageAmlProvider
member.summary:
Production-oriented AML provider adapter for ComplyAdvantage.
Uses the ComplyAdvantage Screening API to screen subjects against sanctions lists, PEP watchlists, and adverse media sources. Supports both individual and business-entity subjects. Business entities use the legal_name, registration_number, and jurisdiction metadata fields.
Fail-closed behaviour: when AmlConfig.ApiKey is missing or blank, every call returns rather than silently approving subjects.
Configure via appsettings.json:
"AmlConfig": { "Provider": "ComplyAdvantage", "ApiKey": "...", // inject via env: AmlConfig__ApiKey "ApiEndpoint": "https://api.complyadvantage.com", "IncludePepScreening": true, "IncludeAdverseMedia": false, "FuzzinessThreshold": 0, "MinApprovalConfidence": 0.8, "EvidenceValidityHours": 720 }

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.DeploymentSignOffService
member.summary:
Validates deployment sign-off readiness and generates structured proof documents for enterprise sign-off journeys.
The service is fail-closed: if any required sign-off criterion cannot be satisfied (missing asset ID, missing transaction ID, non-terminal state, absent audit trail), the proof verdict is and actionable, non-technical guidance is returned so a business operator can understand the problem without needing blockchain expertise.
This service depends on to retrieve deployment state and audit trail.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.DeploymentSignOffService.#ctor(BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IBackendDeploymentLifecycleContractService,Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Services.DeploymentSignOffService})
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.DeploymentStatusService
member.summary: Service for managing deployment status tracking and state transitions
member.remarks: Implements business logic for deployment status tracking including: - State machine validation for status transitions - Idempotency guards to prevent duplicate status updates - Webhook notifications for status changes - Retry logic for transient failures

member.name: F:BiatecTokensApi.Services.DeploymentStatusService.ValidTransitions
member.summary: Valid status transitions in the deployment state machine
member.remarks:
State Machine Flow: Queued → Submitted → Pending → Confirmed → Indexed → Completed ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ ↓ Failed ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← ← (from any non-terminal state) ↓ Queued (retry allowed)
Queued → Cancelled (user-initiated)

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.EnterpriseComplianceReviewService
member.summary:
Repository-backed implementation of the enterprise compliance review service.
Provides role-aware reviewer queues, compliance evidence bundles, structured review decision recording, audit history export, and operational diagnostics for enterprise team operations.
All mutable review state (decisions, diagnostics) is delegated to so that review history survives DI scope changes and can be upgraded to persistent storage by swapping the repository.
This service builds on top of to add: - Evidence bundle synthesis with contradiction detection - Role-capability calculation for frontend permission surfacing - Structured diagnostics for operator observability - Enriched audit history with actor roles and evidence references - Audit export suitable for regulators and auditors
Initializes a new instance of .

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ERC20TokenService
member.summary: Provides functionality for deploying and interacting with ERC-20 token contracts on blockchain networks.
member.remarks: The class is designed to facilitate the deployment of ERC-20 token contracts and manage interactions with the BiatecToken smart contract. It loads the ABI and bytecode for the BiatecToken contract from a JSON file and uses this information to deploy contracts and perform related operations. This service relies on blockchain configuration settings and application-specific settings provided via dependency injection. It also logs relevant information and errors during operations. Ensure that the required ABI and bytecode file ("BiatecToken.json") is present in the "ABI" directory under the application's base directory.

member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class, loading the ABI and bytecode for the BiatecToken contract and configuring the service.
member.remarks: This constructor reads the ABI and bytecode for the BiatecToken contract from a JSON file located in the "ABI" directory under the application's base directory. The loaded ABI and bytecode are used to interact with the BiatecToken smart contract. Ensure that the "BiatecToken.json" file is present and correctly formatted in the expected location.
member.param: The configuration monitor for blockchain-related settings.
The configuration monitor for application-specific settings.
The logger used to log information and errors for this service.
The authentication service for user account management
The user repository for accessing user data
HTTP context accessor for correlation ID extraction
member.exception: Thrown if the BiatecToken contract bytecode is not found in the ABI JSON file.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ERC20TokenService.ValidateRequest(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ERC20.Request.ERC20TokenDeploymentRequest,BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType)
member.summary: Validates the deployment request for an ERC20 token based on the specified token type.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ERC20TokenService.DeployERC20TokenAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ERC20.Request.ERC20TokenDeploymentRequest,BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenType,System.String)
member.summary: Deploys an ERC-20 token contract to the specified blockchain network.
member.remarks: This method deploys an ERC-20 token contract using the provided deployment parameters. The initial supply is allocated to the specified receiver address, or to the deployer's address if no receiver is provided. The method handles exceptions and logs relevant information about the deployment process.
member.param: The deployment request containing the token details, such as name, symbol, decimals, initial supply, and the blockchain configuration (e.g., chain ID and RPC URL).
Optional user ID for JWT-authenticated requests. If provided, uses user's ARC76 account. If null, uses system account.
member.returns: A containing the deployment result, including the contract address, transaction hash, and success status. If the deployment fails, the response includes an error message.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IBackendDeploymentLifecycleContractService
member.summary:
Service contract for deterministic backend deployment lifecycle with ARC76 hardening.
Design guarantees: - Same email+password always derives the same ARC76 Algorand address (determinism). - Replayed requests with identical idempotency keys return cached results (idempotency). - Illegal lifecycle state transitions are blocked with structured error codes. - Every deployment emits compliance audit events with correlation IDs. - Errors are classified into a stable machine-readable taxonomy.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IBackendDeploymentLifecycleContractService.InitiateAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.BackendDeploymentLifecycle.BackendDeploymentContractRequest)
member.summary: Initiates or resumes a deterministic backend deployment contract. Derives the deployer account via ARC76 (or uses an explicit address), validates inputs, enforces lifecycle state transitions, and emits audit events. Replayed requests with the same idempotency key return the cached result.
member.param: Deployment parameters including credentials or explicit address.
member.returns: Deployment contract response with lifecycle state, derivation evidence, and audit trail.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IBackendDeploymentLifecycleContractService.GetStatusAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the current state of a deployment contract by its ID.
member.returns: Current deployment contract response, or a not-found error response.

member.summary: Validates deployment inputs and ARC76 derivation without committing any state. Returns field-level validation results and derivation evidence.
member.param: Validation request with credentials and deployment parameters.
member.returns: Validation response with field results and derivation status.

member.summary: Returns the compliance audit trail for a deployment contract. Includes all audit events emitted across the deployment lifecycle.
member.returns: Compliance audit trail with ordered events and final state.

member.summary: Derives the ARC76 Algorand address from email and password without deploying. Same credentials always produce the same address (determinism contract).
member.returns: Derived Algorand address string.
member.exception: Thrown when email or password is null/whitespace.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceAuditExportService
member.summary: Service interface for assembling and retrieving scenario-specific compliance audit export packages.
member.remarks:
Each package is assembled for a specific scenario (release-readiness, onboarding case review, compliance blocker review, or approval-history export) and audience profile.
Readiness semantics are fail-closed:
Any missing required evidence → Incomplete
Any stale required evidence → Stale
Any unresolved critical blocker → Blocked
Provider unreachable → DegradedProviderUnavailable
Manual review stage pending → RequiresReview
All checks pass → Ready
The backend is the authoritative source for readiness determination. Clients must never synthesise missing evidence or override the readiness status.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceAuditExportService.AssembleReleaseReadinessExportAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ComplianceAuditExport.ReleaseReadinessExportRequest)
member.summary: Assembles a release-readiness sign-off audit export package.
member.param: Request containing the subject ID, audience profile, optional head reference, environment label, and evidence filter timestamp.
member.returns: Assembly response containing the canonical package. If an idempotency key is supplied and a matching package exists, the cached package is returned with IsIdempotentReplay = true.

member.summary: Assembles an onboarding case review audit export package.
member.param: Request containing the subject ID, audience profile, and optional case ID. If no case ID is specified, the most recent onboarding case for the subject is used.
member.returns: Assembly response containing the canonical package.

member.summary: Assembles a compliance blocker review audit export package.

member.returns: Assembly response containing the canonical package.

Covers evidence staleness, missing evidence, unresolved escalations, open remediation tasks, SLA breaches, pending KYC/AML decisions, incomplete approvals, and handoff failures. Fail-closed: when blockers are present, is false.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceCaseManagementService.GetEvidenceAvailabilityAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Computes and returns the case-level evidence availability summary with explicit Complete / Partial / Stale / Unavailable semantics. Designed for direct frontend cockpit consumption — the caller does not need to infer availability from low-level evidence item flags. Automatically checks bundle freshness before computing.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceCaseManagementService.GetOrchestrationViewAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns a comprehensive orchestration view of a compliance case — the single authoritative snapshot needed by the frontend operations cockpit to render role-aware operator journeys. Combines current state, evidence availability, active blockers, SLA metadata, handoff status, available transitions, and next-action guidance in one call. Fail-closed: returns explicit error when the case is not found.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService
member.summary:
Service contract for the compliance operations orchestration layer.
Aggregates signals from scheduled reporting, approval workflows, compliance case management, and ongoing monitoring into a unified SLA-aware queue and overview.
Design principles: - Fail-closed: missing evidence, failed delivery, or unresolved approvals always produce explicit non-healthy states rather than silently appearing healthy. - Deterministic SLA classification: OnTrack / DueSoon / Overdue / Blocked with numeric ordering so comparisons are stable. - Role-aware aggregation: ComplianceManager, OperationsLead, ExecutiveSummary each receive tailored views without bespoke endpoints. - Webhook emission: overdue and blocked state transitions emit audit events for enterprise subscriber consumption.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService.GetOverviewAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns a cross-workflow compliance operations overview with aggregated counts, per-role summaries, health status, and blocker breakdowns.
member.param: Identity of the requesting actor (for audit trail).

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService.GetQueueAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ComplianceOperations.ComplianceOpsQueueRequest,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Returns a prioritised, filterable compliance operations queue.
Supports role-based views (ComplianceManager, OperationsLead, ExecutiveSummary), workflow-type filtering, SLA status filtering, and fail-closed-only filtering. Items are ordered by PriorityScore descending (blocked > overdue > due-soon > on-track).

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService.UpsertQueueItemAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ComplianceOperations.ComplianceOpsQueueItem,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Adds or updates a queue item in the in-memory store. Emits webhook events when the SLA status transitions to Overdue or Blocked. Designed to be called by other services when they record new workflow state.
member.param: The queue item to register or update.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService.ResolveQueueItemAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Marks a queue item as resolved and removes it from the active queue. Emits a event.
member.param: Stable identifier of the item to resolve.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService.ClassifySlaStatus(System.Nullable{System.DateTime},System.DateTime)
member.summary:
Classifies a due date and current time into a .
Rules: - No deadline → OnTrack - Now < DueAt − 3 days → OnTrack - DueAt − 3 days ≤ Now ≤ DueAt → DueSoon - Now > DueAt → Overdue - Explicit blocker → Blocked (caller responsibility)

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceOperationsService.ComputePriorityScore(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ComplianceOperations.ComplianceOpsSlaStatus,System.Boolean,System.DateTime,System.DateTime)
member.summary: Computes a priority score for queue ordering. Blocked items always outrank Overdue, which outrank DueSoon, which outrank OnTrack. Within a tier, fail-closed items score higher than non-fail-closed items. Within that, older items (larger age) score higher to prevent starvation.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceService.GenerateComplianceEvidenceBundleAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.Compliance.GenerateComplianceEvidenceBundleRequest,System.String)
member.summary: Generates a signed compliance evidence bundle (ZIP) for auditors
member.param: The evidence bundle request containing asset ID and date range
The address of the user requesting the bundle
member.returns: Response with the ZIP bundle content and manifest metadata
member.remarks:
This method generates a comprehensive ZIP archive containing: - Audit logs (whitelist, blacklist, compliance operations) - Whitelist history and current entries - Transfer approval records - Token metadata and compliance policies - Manifest file with SHA256 checksums and timestamps
The bundle is designed for MICA/RWA compliance audits and supports external verification with cryptographic checksums.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IComplianceService.GetDashboardAggregationAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.Compliance.GetComplianceDashboardAggregationRequest,System.String)
member.summary: Gets aggregated compliance dashboard metrics for enterprise reporting
member.param: The dashboard aggregation request with filters
The address of the user requesting the dashboard data
member.returns: Response with aggregated compliance metrics including MICA readiness, whitelist status, and jurisdiction flags
member.remarks:
This method aggregates compliance data across assets to provide: - MICA readiness statistics (compliant, nearly compliant, in progress, non-compliant) - Whitelist status metrics (enabled/disabled, active/revoked/suspended addresses) - Jurisdiction coverage (distribution, unique jurisdictions) - Compliant vs restricted asset counts - Top restriction reasons with occurrence counts - Token standard and network distribution
Supports filtering by network, token standard, and date range for targeted reporting. Designed for enterprise compliance dashboards and scheduled compliance exports.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentEvidenceProvider
member.summary:
Provides blockchain deployment evidence for a completed token deployment transaction.
This interface is the key abstraction that separates authoritative production deployments (where evidence is obtained from a live blockchain node) from simulated test deployments (where evidence is derived deterministically for contract-shape validation).
Implementations:
— returns deterministic hash-derived evidence; sets = true. Suitable for development, testing, and non-production environments.
— queries a live Algorand indexer node; sets IsSimulated = false. Required for production regulated issuance sign-off on Algorand-family networks. Enabled by setting DeploymentEvidenceConfig:Provider = "Algorand".
The calls this provider during the Submitting/Confirming lifecycle stages. If the provider returns null (meaning authoritative evidence is unavailable) and the request is in mode, the service transitions the deployment to with and error code BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentEvidenceProvider.ObtainEvidenceAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Obtains or derives blockchain evidence for the specified deployment. Returns null when authoritative evidence cannot be obtained (e.g. node unreachable, timeout, or this provider is intentionally fail-closed for testing).
member.param: The deterministic deployment ID derived from the idempotency key and network.
Token standard (e.g., "ASA", "ARC3", "ARC200", "ERC20") used to format standard-specific evidence fields.
member.returns: when evidence is available; null when evidence cannot be obtained.

member.name: P:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentEvidenceProvider.IsSimulated
member.summary: true when this provider always returns simulated (non-authoritative) evidence. false when this provider attempts to obtain confirmed blockchain state.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentSignOffService
member.summary:
Service that validates deployment sign-off readiness and generates structured proof documents for enterprise sign-off journeys.
The sign-off service is the backend's authoritative check that a deployment lifecycle has completed with all required evidence fields present. It evaluates each criterion in a fail-closed manner: if any required criterion fails, sign-off is blocked and actionable guidance is returned so a non-technical operator can understand the problem and take corrective action.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentSignOffService.ValidateSignOffReadinessAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.DeploymentSignOff.DeploymentSignOffValidationRequest)
member.summary:
Validates whether a deployment is ready for enterprise sign-off.
Evaluates all configured sign-off criteria against the deployment record. Missing required fields (AssetId, TransactionId, ConfirmedRound, DeployerAddress) are treated as explicit failures, not silently ignored.
member.param: Validation request specifying the deployment ID and which criteria are required.
member.returns: A structured proof document with per-criterion outcomes, an overall verdict, and actionable guidance for any failures.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentSignOffService.GenerateSignOffProofAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Generates a sign-off proof document for a deployment without an explicit criteria request. Uses default criteria (all required).
member.param: Deployment contract ID to generate proof for.
member.returns: Structured sign-off proof document.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService
member.summary:
Service interface for enterprise compliance review operations.
Provides role-aware reviewer queues, compliance evidence bundles, structured review decision recording, audit history, audit export, and operational diagnostics.
Authorization is enforced at every operation: - Actors must be active members of the issuer team. - Role-specific capabilities (Approve, Reject, etc.) require the appropriate role. - Authorization failures are fail-closed with explicit operator guidance.
Tenant isolation is enforced at all times: one issuer cannot access another issuer's review data, evidence bundles, or diagnostics.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService.GetReviewQueueAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Returns the role-filtered reviewer queue for an issuer. Items are enriched with the actor's available capabilities and evidence readiness summary.
- Operators/Admins see items they created that need action (NeedsChanges, Prepared). - Reviewers/Approvers/Admins see items pending their review (PendingReview). - ReadOnlyObserver/all roles see all items they are permitted to view.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService.GetEvidenceBundleAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Assembles a compliance evidence bundle for a specific workflow review item.
The bundle includes: - Supporting evidence items with validation status and rationale. - Detected contradictions between evidence sources. - Missing evidence indicators (blocking and non-blocking). - Overall review readiness assessment.
Returns an explicit "incomplete review" state when blocking issues exist, rather than implying readiness.
member.param: Workflow item to assemble evidence for.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService.SubmitReviewDecisionAsync(System.String,System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.EnterpriseComplianceReview.SubmitReviewDecisionRequest,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Submits a structured review decision (Approve, Reject, or RequestChanges) for a workflow item.
Enforces role-based authorization: - Approve/Reject/RequestChanges: requires ComplianceReviewer, FinanceReviewer, or Admin role.
Validates state machine rules: - The item must be in PendingReview state. - Invalid transitions fail loudly with a structured error.
Approvals with open critical contradictions require explicit acknowledgement. Records the decision in the workflow audit trail with structured rationale and evidence references.
member.param: The decision with rationale and evidence references.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService.GetAuditHistoryAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Returns the full enriched audit history for a workflow item.
Each entry includes the actor's role at the time of the action, structured rationale, evidence references, and correlation IDs for distributed tracing. Suitable for audit reconstruction and compliance reporting.
member.param: Workflow item to retrieve history for.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService.ExportAuditAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.EnterpriseComplianceReview.ReviewAuditExportRequest,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Exports workflow review decisions for an issuer in a structured format.
Supports filtering by time range, state, item type, and actor. Output is structured for regulatory review, auditors, or internal compliance reporting. Suitable as input for CSV or JSON export from the frontend.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IEnterpriseComplianceReviewService.GetDiagnosticsAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Returns structured diagnostics for an issuer's compliance review operations.
Surfaces: - Authorization denial events. - Invalid workflow transition attempts. - Missing evidence situations. - Evidence contradiction detections. - Stale items that have not progressed in an extended period.
Designed for operator visibility and support investigation, not for end-user display. Requires Admin or ComplianceReviewer role.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IIssuerWorkflowService
member.summary:
Service interface for issuer-scoped team roles, membership management, and workflow approval-state operations.
Two layers of protection are enforced: 1. Tenant isolation – every method scopes results to issuerId so one issuer cannot read or mutate another issuer's data. 2. Membership authorization – the acting user (actorId) must be an active member of the issuer; sensitive operations additionally require a specific role (see individual method docs and ).
Bootstrap behaviour: when an issuer has no members at all, any authenticated caller may add the first member. That first member must be an Admin so they can subsequently manage the team.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService
member.summary:
Manages the full lifecycle of provider-backed KYC/AML onboarding cases.
Each case represents a single subject's onboarding journey from initial creation through provider checks, reviewer decisions, and a final approved or rejected outcome. The state machine is fail-closed: if the required provider is absent, all operations surface that explicitly.
All methods are idempotent-safe where idempotency keys are supplied and record a full audit trail of reviewer actions with actor IDs and rationale.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService.CreateCaseAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.KycAmlOnboarding.CreateOnboardingCaseRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Creates a new KYC/AML onboarding case for the given subject.
If an idempotency key is supplied and a case already exists for the same SubjectId and key, the existing case is returned without modification.
member.param: ID of the operator creating the case.
member.returns: The created (or idempotently returned) case.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService.GetCaseAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Returns the current state of a single onboarding case.
member.param: ID of the case to retrieve.
member.returns: The case record, or a not-found error response.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService.InitiateProviderChecksAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.KycAmlOnboarding.InitiateProviderChecksRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Initiates provider KYC/AML checks for an onboarding case.
When the required provider service is not configured, the response carries ErrorCode = "PROVIDER_NOT_CONFIGURED" and the case state is set to . This is an explicit fail-closed outcome — there is no silent success-shaped fallback.
If the case is not in the state, the response carries ErrorCode = "INVALID_STATE".
member.param: ID of the case to advance.
ID of the operator initiating checks.
member.returns: Updated case record and verification journey ID on success.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService.RecordReviewerActionAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.KycAmlOnboarding.RecordReviewerActionRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Records a reviewer action on an onboarding case and applies the corresponding state transition.
Valid state transitions:
: PendingReview/UnderReview → Approved
: any non-terminal → Rejected
: PendingReview/UnderReview → Escalated
: PendingReview/UnderReview → RequiresAdditionalInfo
: any active state, no state change
Invalid transitions return ErrorCode = "INVALID_STATE_TRANSITION".
member.param: ID of the case to act on.
Action parameters including kind, rationale, and notes.
ID of the reviewer taking the action.
member.returns: The recorded action and updated case record.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService.GetEvidenceSummaryAsync(System.String)
member.summary:
Returns a structured evidence summary for a case, reflecting provider backing status, checks completed, and actionable guidance.
When provider configuration is absent, the summary carries and IsProviderBacked = false. There is no silent "green" fallback.
member.param: ID of the case to summarise.
member.returns: Evidence summary or a not-found error response.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IKycAmlOnboardingCaseService.ListCasesAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.KycAmlOnboarding.ListOnboardingCasesRequest)
member.summary: Lists onboarding cases, optionally filtered by subject ID and state.
member.returns: A page of matching cases with a total count.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ILiveProviderVerificationJourneyService
member.summary:
Orchestrates end-to-end live-provider KYC/AML verification journeys for subject onboarding and compliance case workflows.
This service provides a unified, operator-observable view of a subject's complete verification journey — from KYC initiation through AML screening, manual review, and final approval decision — with structured diagnostics, approval-decision observability, and release-grade evidence generation.
All methods fail closed when required provider configuration is absent, a provider is unreachable, or evidence is stale. There are no silent success-shaped fallbacks for degraded or misconfigured provider paths.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ILiveProviderVerificationJourneyService.StartJourneyAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.LiveProviderVerificationJourney.StartVerificationJourneyRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Starts a new live-provider KYC/AML verification journey for a subject.
Initiates the configured KYC identity check and (if applicable) AML screening against the requested execution mode. Creates a durable journey record with structured diagnostics.
When is true, the call fails if execution mode resolves to Simulated. When provider configuration is missing or the provider is unreachable, the journey is created in the stage with actionable diagnostics — it does not silently continue.
member.param: Journey start parameters including subject ID and metadata.
ID of the operator or system initiating the journey.
member.returns: The created journey record and diagnostics.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ILiveProviderVerificationJourneyService.GetJourneyStatusAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Returns the current status of a verification journey, including the full step audit trail and a computed approval-decision explanation.
The approval-decision explanation includes structured rationale for why the journey is at its current stage, what checks passed or failed, and what action the operator should take next.
member.param: ID of the verification journey to query.
Optional correlation ID for distributed tracing.
member.returns: Journey status with approval decision explanation.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ILiveProviderVerificationJourneyService.EvaluateApprovalDecisionAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary:
Evaluates and returns the current approval-decision explanation for a verification journey.
Provides structured operator-facing rationale for why a journey is approved, rejected, pending review, blocked, or requiring action. Includes: - which checks passed, failed, or are pending; - whether evidence is provider-backed and release-grade; - actionable guidance for each non-approved state.
member.param: ID of the verification journey to evaluate.
Optional correlation ID for distributed tracing.
member.returns: Approval decision explanation with structured diagnostics.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ILiveProviderVerificationJourneyService.GenerateReleaseEvidenceAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.LiveProviderVerificationJourney.GenerateVerificationJourneyEvidenceRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Generates a durable, content-hashed release evidence artifact for a verification journey.
The artifact bundles all journey steps, the current approval-decision explanation, and provider references into a single tamper-evident record suitable for business-owner sign-off, audit, and regulator-facing review.
When is true, generation fails if the journey execution mode is Simulated. This preserves the fail-closed contract for protected release paths.
member.param: Evidence generation parameters including release tag.
member.returns: Release evidence artifact or failure diagnostics.

member.summary: Starts a reassessment on the specified monitoring task. Transitions the task from Healthy, DueSoon, or Overdue to InProgress. Fails if the task is already in a terminal state (Resolved, Suspended, Restricted).

member.summary: Escalates a monitoring task for senior review. Can optionally raise the severity. EscalationReason must be non-empty. Fails if the task is already in a terminal state.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IOngoingMonitoringService.CloseTaskAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.OngoingMonitoring.CloseMonitoringTaskRequest,System.String)
member.summary: Closes a monitoring task with an auditable resolution outcome. ResolutionNotes must be non-empty. Terminal states (Resolved, Suspended, Restricted) cannot be re-closed.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IOngoingMonitoringService.RunDueDateCheckAsync
member.summary: Scans all open monitoring tasks and marks those whose DueAt is in the past as Overdue. Also advances Deferred tasks back to DueSoon or Overdue when their DeferredUntil has elapsed. Returns a count of tasks updated.
