member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService
member.summary: Service that delivers protected sign-off environment readiness, enterprise lifecycle verification, fixture provisioning, and operational diagnostics needed to run a credible, fail-closed protected sign-off against a live backend.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService.CheckEnvironmentReadinessAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOff.ProtectedSignOffEnvironmentRequest)
member.summary: Checks whether the protected sign-off environment is ready for a protected run. Verifies that all required backend services, configuration, and infrastructure are available and operational.
member.param: Request specifying which checks to perform.
member.returns: A describing the readiness status of each environment component and actionable guidance when remediation is required.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService.ExecuteSignOffLifecycleAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOff.EnterpriseSignOffLifecycleRequest)
member.summary: Executes a deterministic verification of every stage in the enterprise sign-off lifecycle journey (authentication → initiation → status polling → terminal state → validation). Each stage verifies that the backend API contract is stable and produces actionable evidence for protected sign-off.
member.param: Request specifying the issuer, deployment, and lifecycle options.
member.returns: A with per-stage results and a top-level flag indicating whether the full lifecycle is ready for protected evidence.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService.ProvisionEnterpriseFixturesAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOff.EnterpriseFixtureProvisionRequest)
member.summary: Provisions deterministic enterprise fixtures (issuer team and sign-off user) so the protected sign-off journey can execute against realistic authorization state without relying on ad-hoc runtime mutation or permissive fallbacks.
member.param: Request specifying the issuer ID, admin user, and reset options.
member.returns: A describing whether provisioning succeeded, whether fixtures already existed, and guidance when it failed.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService.GetDiagnosticsAsync(System.String)
member.summary: Gathers operational diagnostics for the protected sign-off backend, distinguishing configuration failures, authorization failures, contract failures, and lifecycle failures so that operators can triage protected-run problems quickly.
member.param: Optional correlation ID for distributed tracing.
member.returns: A with structured failure-category information and per-service availability status.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService
member.summary:
Service contract for protected sign-off evidence persistence and approval-webhook parity.
This service provides authoritative backend storage and querying of approval webhook outcomes, protected sign-off evidence packs, and aggregated release-readiness status for the current head.
All methods are fail-closed: when required inputs are missing, evidence is stale, or approval has not been received, the service produces explicit blocked or unavailable states rather than optimistic partial success.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService.RecordApprovalWebhookAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistence.RecordApprovalWebhookRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Records an incoming approval or escalation webhook outcome and persists it against the specified compliance case and head ref.
Malformed payloads are recorded with rather than silently dropped, enabling operators to diagnose webhook delivery failures.
member.param: The webhook arrival details including outcome, case ID, and head ref.
The ID of the operator or system recording this webhook.
member.returns: A persisted or a failure response with diagnostics.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService.PersistSignOffEvidenceAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistence.PersistSignOffEvidenceRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Persists a protected sign-off evidence pack for the specified head ref, capturing the current state of all required evidence items.
When is true, the call fails if the evidence pack cannot be marked as release-grade. When is true, the call fails if no approval webhook has been received for this head ref.
member.param: The ID of the operator triggering evidence capture.
member.returns: The persisted evidence pack or a failure response with diagnostics.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService.GetReleaseReadinessAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistence.GetSignOffReleaseReadinessRequest)
member.summary:
Evaluates and returns the aggregated release-readiness status for the specified head ref.
The response is a single authoritative object that the frontend can consume without stitching together multiple weak signals. It includes evidence freshness, approval webhook presence, ordered blockers with remediation hints, and top-level operator guidance.
The method is fail-closed: it reports when required approvals or evidence are absent, and when evidence has expired or was produced for a different head.
member.returns: Aggregated release readiness status with blockers and operator guidance.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService.GetApprovalWebhookHistoryAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistence.GetApprovalWebhookHistoryRequest)
member.summary: Retrieves the approval webhook history filtered by optional case ID and head ref.
member.returns: Ordered history of approval webhook records (newest first).

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService.GetEvidencePackHistoryAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistence.GetEvidencePackHistoryRequest)
member.summary: Retrieves the evidence pack history filtered by optional head ref and case ID.
member.returns: Ordered history of evidence packs (newest first).

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProviderBackedComplianceExecutionService
member.summary:
Service contract for provider-backed compliance case execution.
This service bridges the compliance case lifecycle (approve / reject / RFI / sanctions review) with external provider validation, enforces fail-closed behaviour for protected paths, and produces durable, release-grade evidence artifacts that business owners and compliance leads can review.
All methods fail clearly when required configuration is absent or a provider is unavailable — there are no silent success-shaped fallbacks.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProviderBackedComplianceExecutionService.ExecuteDecisionAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProviderBackedCompliance.ExecuteProviderBackedDecisionRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Executes a compliance case decision (approve, reject, return-for-information, sanctions review, or escalation) through the provider-backed execution path.
When is true, the call fails if execution mode is Simulated.
When is true and execution mode is LiveProvider or ProtectedSandbox, the call fails if KYC/AML sign-off evidence for the case subject is absent, stale, or not provider-backed.
member.param: ID of the compliance case to act on.
ID of the operator or system initiating the decision.
member.returns: Execution result including evidence artifact and diagnostics.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProviderBackedComplianceExecutionService.GetExecutionStatusAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the current execution status and full execution history for a compliance case, including diagnostics from the most recent attempt.
member.param: Optional correlation ID for distributed tracing.
member.returns: Execution status and ordered history.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IProviderBackedComplianceExecutionService.BuildSignOffEvidenceAsync(System.String,BiatecTokensApi.Models.ProviderBackedCompliance.BuildProviderBackedSignOffEvidenceRequest,System.String)
member.summary:
Builds a durable, content-hashed sign-off evidence bundle for a compliance case.
When is true, the build fails if the execution history contains any simulated executions.
member.returns: Sign-off evidence bundle or failure diagnostics.

member.summary: Service interface for assembling and retrieving regulator-facing evidence packages.
member.remarks: Each package has a stable identifier, an audience profile, and a manifest that enumerates every source record considered during assembly — including those that are missing or stale. The backend is the authoritative source for readiness determination; clients must never synthesise missing evidence or override the readiness status.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IScheduledReportingService
member.summary:
Service interface for managing scheduled compliance reporting templates, report runs, schedule definitions, delivery tracking, and approval workflows.
Design principles: - Fail-closed: if required evidence is missing or stale, runs return blocked status. - Auditability: every template and run records who created it, who approved it, and what evidence set was used. - Background-execution-ready: service is designed so report generation can be offloaded to background workers without altering the API contract.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ITokenDeploymentLifecycleService
member.summary:
Service interface for the deterministic token deployment lifecycle.
Provides idempotent deployment initiation, progress tracking, retry handling, telemetry emission, input validation, and reliability guardrail evaluation.
All operations are deterministic: identical inputs always produce identical results. Retried operations are safe and do not create duplicate resources.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ITokenDeploymentLifecycleService.InitiateDeploymentAsync(BiatecTokensApi.Models.TokenDeploymentLifecycle.TokenDeploymentLifecycleRequest)
member.summary: Initiates a new token deployment or returns a cached result for an idempotent replay. Validates inputs, evaluates reliability guardrails, and transitions the deployment through its lifecycle stages.
member.param: Deployment request including token parameters and idempotency key.
member.returns: Deployment lifecycle response with stage, idempotency status, telemetry, and guardrails.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ITokenDeploymentLifecycleService.GetDeploymentStatusAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns the current status of a deployment by its deployment ID.
member.returns: Current lifecycle response, or a not-found response when the ID is unknown.

member.summary: Retries a previously failed or timed-out deployment. Honours the configured MaxRetryAttempts limit and returns a terminal failure response when the limit has been exceeded.
member.param: Retry request including the original idempotency key.
member.returns: Updated deployment lifecycle response.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ITokenDeploymentLifecycleService.GetTelemetryEventsAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Returns all telemetry events emitted during the lifecycle of a deployment. Events include stage transitions, guardrail triggers, idempotency hits, and dependency failures.
member.param: Deployment ID to retrieve telemetry for.
member.returns: Ordered list of telemetry events.

member.returns: Validation response with field-level results and guardrail findings.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ITokenWorkflowOrchestrationPipeline
member.summary: Orchestration pipeline for policy-driven token workflow execution.
member.remarks:
Encapsulates the five-stage pipeline: 1. Validate – schema and invariant checks 2. CheckPreconditions – KYC, subscription, compliance policy gates 3. Execute – core token operation (idempotency-guarded) 4. VerifyPostCommit – confirm operation reached expected terminal state 5. EmitTelemetry – structured lifecycle events with correlation ID and stage markers
Every run produces an that carries full stage markers, policy decisions, and an audit summary regardless of outcome.

member.summary: Executes a token workflow through the full five-stage pipeline.
member.typeparam: Type of the operation result payload
member.param: Orchestration context carrying correlation ID, idempotency key, and user identity
Delegate that validates the request at Stage 1. Return null to indicate success; return a non-null error message to halt the pipeline.
Delegate that checks preconditions at Stage 2. Return null to proceed; return a non-null error message to halt the pipeline.
Async delegate that executes the core operation at Stage 3. Should throw on unrecoverable failures; transient failures are caught and classified.
Optional async delegate that verifies the result at Stage 4. Return null to indicate verification passed; return a non-null message on failure.
Cancellation token for the pipeline execution
member.returns: A fully populated

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ITokenWorkflowOrchestrationPipeline.BuildContext(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Builds an from the provided values. Callers should pass the correlation ID obtained from HttpContext.TraceIdentifier and the idempotency key obtained from the Idempotency-Key request header.
member.param: Correlation ID from the request trace identifier

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.IssuanceRiskScoringService
member.summary: Deterministic compliance risk scoring service for token issuance workflows.
member.remarks:
Computes a normalized aggregate risk score (0–100) from three independent components:
KYC completeness and verification status (0–40 points)
Sanctions screening outcome and confidence (0–30 points)
Jurisdiction risk level (0–30 points)
Risk band thresholds (deterministic, configuration-independent defaults):
0–39 → Low → decision: allow
40–69 → Medium → decision: review
70–100 → High → decision: deny

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlDecisionIngestionService
member.summary:
In-memory implementation of .
Provides provider-agnostic ingestion of KYC/AML compliance decisions, evidence retention with provenance, and fail-closed readiness aggregation.
Fail-closed rules:
ProviderUnavailable, Error, or Contradiction → Blocked (hard).
Rejected or InsufficientData → Blocked (hard).
Expired evidence → Stale (hard) if a decision is marked expired.
NeedsReview → PendingReview (blocks Ready; not blocked yet).
All decisions Approved and no evidence expired → Ready.
No decisions present for a subject → EvidenceMissing (hard).

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlDecisionIngestionService.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlDecisionIngestionService},System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Initialises the service with an optional time provider (defaults to system time). Injecting a test-controlled allows deterministic freshness and expiry tests without Thread.Sleep.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlDecisionIngestionService.ComputeSubjectReadiness(System.String,System.Collections.Generic.List{BiatecTokensApi.Models.KycAmlDecisionIngestion.IngestionDecisionRecord})
member.summary:
Applies fail-closed readiness rules to a subject's decision set. Rules evaluated in priority order (most severe first):
No decisions → EvidenceMissing (blocked).
ProviderUnavailable → Blocked.
Error → Blocked.
Contradiction → Blocked.
Rejected or InsufficientData → Blocked.
Expired evidence (IsEvidenceExpired) → Stale (blocked).
NeedsReview → PendingReview.
All Approved, no expired evidence → Ready.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlOnboardingCaseService
member.summary:
Provider-backed KYC/AML onboarding case lifecycle service.
Manages cases from initial creation through provider checks, reviewer decisions, and a final approved or rejected outcome. State transitions are strictly enforced; invalid transitions and absent provider configuration surface explicitly — there are no silent success-shaped fallbacks.
Storage is in-process via ; a persistent backing store can be substituted without changing the API contract.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlOnboardingCaseService.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlOnboardingCaseService},BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.ILiveProviderVerificationJourneyService,BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IWebhookService,System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Initialises the onboarding case service.
member.param: Optional live-provider journey service. When null, provider-check operations fail closed with .
Optional webhook service for event emission.
Substitutable time provider for deterministic testing.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.KycAmlSignOffEvidenceService
member.summary:
Orchestrates provider-backed KYC/AML sign-off evidence flows.
This service wraps the underlying KYC and AML provider adapters, tracks execution mode (live, protected sandbox, or simulated), generates durable evidence artifacts, provides plain-language explanations for enterprise frontend workflows, and enforces fail-closed behaviour for all adverse or degraded conditions.
Fail-closed rules (all produce a blocked readiness state, never silent approval):
Provider unavailable / timeout →
Malformed or missing callback data →
Adverse findings (sanctions, PEP, etc.) → or
Stale / expired evidence →
Incomplete remediation →
Unknown provider reference → callback rejected
Initialises the service.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.MockAmlProvider
member.summary:
Mock AML provider for testing and development. Behaviour is controlled via the subjectMetadata dictionary:
sanctions_flag=true → Rejected (reason: SANCTIONS_MATCH)
review_flag=true → NeedsReview (reason: REVIEW_REQUIRED)
simulate_timeout=true → Error / Timeout
simulate_unavailable=true → ProviderUnavailable (fail-closed)
simulate_malformed=true → Error / MalformedResponse
simulate_insufficient_data=true → InsufficientData
(none of the above) → Approved

member.summary:

All read operations are idempotent – repeated calls with the same inputs return semantically identical responses, supporting safe retries and stable contract behaviour for frontend and enterprise consumers.
Internal implementation details and stack traces are never surfaced in public-facing payloads; only bounded error codes and remediation hints are exposed.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService
member.summary:
Delivers protected sign-off environment readiness checks, enterprise lifecycle verification, fixture provisioning, and operational diagnostics required to run a credible, fail-closed protected sign-off against a live backend.
This service is the backend foundation for repeatable, enterprise-grade sign-off evidence. It does not weaken security, introduce fake-pass paths, or rely on permissive fallbacks. Every response is deterministic and fail-closed.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ProtectedSignOffEnvironmentService.CheckComplianceWorkflowServices
member.summary: Returns environment checks for the compliance workflow services: KYC/AML sign-off evidence and compliance case management. Both services are required for the full enterprise onboarding journey. A DegradedFail is emitted when a service is absent so that legacy protected runs without compliance wiring are not blocked; a critical failure would prevent a run entirely, which is too strict for a service that is wired up via optional DI injection in the current design.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService
member.summary:
Protected sign-off evidence persistence service.
This service provides authoritative backend storage and querying of approval webhook outcomes, protected sign-off evidence packs, and aggregated release-readiness status for the current head.
⚠ DURABILITY NOTICE — MVP Milestone Scope:
This implementation uses ConcurrentDictionary/ConcurrentQueue in-process volatile storage. All persisted evidence and webhook records are lost on process restart. This is an explicit MVP scope decision and is acceptable for development milestone validation.
Implications for operators: Evidence packs and approval webhook records do NOT survive application restarts. In-flight sign-off workflows must be re-submitted after any restart. The readiness APIs will return Indeterminate or Blocked (with a MissingEvidence blocker) after a restart until new evidence is ingested for the current head.
Migration path: Replace _webhookHistory and _evidencePacks dictionaries with a durable provider (database, distributed cache, event store) in a subsequent milestone. The service interface and domain model are designed to be storage-agnostic.
Fail-closed rules (all produce a non-success response with diagnostics):
Null or empty request → Failed with validation error.
RequireApprovalWebhook=true but no approved webhook for this head → Blocked.
RequireReleaseGrade=true but evidence is not provider-backed → Blocked.
Evidence freshness window exceeded → Stale.
Evidence pack head ref does not match request head ref → HeadMismatch.
Approval webhook outcome is Denied → Blocked with ApprovalDenied blocker.
No evidence after process restart → Blocked/Indeterminate (in-memory only).

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Services.ProtectedSignOffEvidencePersistenceService},BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IWebhookService,System.TimeProvider)
member.summary: Initialises the service.
member.param: Optional webhook service for event emission.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.ProviderBackedComplianceExecutionService
member.summary:
Provider-backed compliance case execution service.
This service bridges the compliance case lifecycle (approve / reject / return-for-information / sanctions review / escalation) with external provider validation, enforces fail-closed behaviour for protected paths, and produces durable, release-grade evidence artifacts.
Fail-closed rules (all produce a non-success response with diagnostics, never a silent approval):
RequireProviderBacked=true but ExecutionMode=Simulated → ConfigurationMissing
RequireKycAmlSignOff=true but sign-off evidence absent/stale → InsufficientEvidence
Case not found → Failed with actionable guidance
Invalid state transition → InvalidState with prior/target state info
Underlying service throws → Failed with structured diagnostics
Initialises the service.
member.param: Optional KYC/AML sign-off evidence service for provider validation.
Substitutable time provider (inject fake for tests).
Optional webhook service for event emission.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.RegulatoryEvidencePackageService
member.summary: In-memory implementation of .
member.remarks:
Assembles regulator-facing evidence packages by composing simulated (or future real) KYC/AML decisions, compliance case records, approval workflow history, and readiness posture transitions into a deterministic, manifest-backed package.
Readiness is fail-closed: - Any missing required source → Incomplete (downgrade) - Any stale required source → Stale (downgrade) - Any unresolved contradiction → Blocked (downgrade, highest priority) - RequiresReview approval stage pending → RequiresReview - All sources available and current → Ready
Audience profiles affect framing and applied rules only. The canonical detail payload is never redacted even for ExecutiveSignOff.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.RetryPolicyClassifier
member.summary: Service for classifying errors into retry policies with deterministic rules
member.remarks:
Implements enterprise-grade retry logic that classifies errors into categories with explicit retry semantics. This enables: 1. Deterministic retry behavior across the platform 2. Clear user guidance on error remediation 3. Optimal balance between availability and avoiding cascade failures
Business Value: Reduces user abandonment by automatically retrying transient failures while providing clear guidance for permanent errors. Improves platform reliability perception and reduces support burden.

is always true. Sign-off tooling MUST check this flag before treating the evidence as production-valid.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.StateTransitionGuard
member.summary: Service for validating deployment state transitions with business rule invariants
member.remarks:
Implements strict state machine validation with explicit invariant checks. This ensures deployment lifecycle remains predictable and auditable.
Business Invariants: 1. Terminal states (Completed, Cancelled) cannot transition to any state 2. Confirmed status requires ConfirmedRound to be set 3. Submitted status requires TransactionHash to be set 4. Failed state can only retry to Queued 5. Status history must be chronologically ordered
Business Value: Prevents invalid state corruption that could lead to lost deployment tracking, incorrect billing, or compliance violations.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.StripeIdentityKycProvider
member.summary:
Production-oriented KYC provider adapter for Stripe Identity.
Uses the Stripe Identity API to create and retrieve identity verification sessions. Supports both individual and business-entity subjects via the subject_type metadata field in the verification session.
Fail-closed behaviour: when KycConfig.ApiKey is missing or blank, every call returns with an explanatory error rather than silently allowing subjects through.
Configure via appsettings.json:
"KycConfig": { "Provider": "StripeIdentity", "ApiKey": "sk_live_...", // inject via env: KycConfig__ApiKey "ApiEndpoint": "https://api.stripe.com", "WebhookSecret": "whsec_...", // inject via env: KycConfig__WebhookSecret "RequestTimeoutSeconds": 30 }

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.TokenDeploymentLifecycleService
member.summary:
Implements the deterministic token deployment lifecycle with idempotency, telemetry emission, and reliability guardrail evaluation.
Design principles: - Identical inputs always produce identical outputs (determinism). - Repeated requests with the same idempotency key never create duplicate resources. - Partial upstream failures produce degraded-mode responses, not hard errors. - Every significant state transition is recorded as a telemetry event. - Guardrail checks run before submission to prevent invariant violations.
Evidence modes: - : the service requires confirmed blockchain evidence from the injected . If the provider returns null, the deployment fails with BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE. This is the default mode and the only one acceptable for production regulated issuance sign-off. - : the service accepts hash-derived evidence from the provider when authoritative data is unavailable, marking the response IsSimulatedEvidence = true. Suitable for testing and development.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.TokenDeploymentLifecycleService.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{BiatecTokensApi.Services.TokenDeploymentLifecycleService},BiatecTokensApi.Services.Interface.IDeploymentEvidenceProvider)
member.summary: Initialises a new instance of .
member.param: Provider for blockchain deployment evidence. Required; inject via DI. for development and test environments. For production regulated issuance, inject a real blockchain provider (e.g. AlgorandDeploymentEvidenceProvider) that obtains confirmed on-chain data and sets = false. When null is passed (for backward compatibility only), defaults to .

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.TokenWorkflowOrchestrationPipeline
member.summary: Implementation of the policy-driven token workflow orchestration pipeline.
member.remarks:
Executes five deterministic stages for every token workflow request:
Stage 1 – Validate: Schema and invariant checks (deterministic, synchronous) Stage 2 – CheckPreconditions: KYC, subscription tier, compliance policy gates Stage 3 – Execute: Core operation with idempotency tracking Stage 4 – VerifyPostCommit: Confirm result reached expected state Stage 5 – EmitTelemetry: Structured lifecycle events with correlation ID
Every run, successful or failed, produces a structured with stage markers, policy decisions, and an audit summary for compliance evidence.
Business value: - Operators can identify exactly which stage failed and why using the stage markers. - Duplicate submissions are detected via idempotency key tracking and return deterministic responses. - Failure categories drive retry guidance without requiring callers to parse raw exceptions. - Audit summaries provide compliance evidence for completed and failed workflows.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Services.UnavailableDeploymentEvidenceProvider
member.summary:
A fail-closed evidence provider that always returns null, simulating the scenario where a live blockchain node is unreachable or no authoritative evidence can be obtained.
This provider is used in tests to verify that the transitions a deployment to with and error code BLOCKCHAIN_EVIDENCE_UNAVAILABLE when operating in mode.
In production, this would be replaced by a real network-aware provider (e.g. AlgorandDeploymentEvidenceProvider) that calls the Algorand indexer or algod node and returns null when the node is unreachable or the transaction has not been confirmed within the configured timeout.

member.name: M:BiatecTokensApi.Services.WalletConnectionService.ValidateWalletAddress(System.String,System.String)
member.remarks: This performs structural validation only (length and character set for Algorand; prefix and hex characters for EVM). It does not verify the Algorand 4-byte checksum or the EVM EIP-55 mixed-case checksum. Use this for fast client-side format checking; rely on on-chain confirmation for authoritative validity.

member.name: T:BiatecTokensApi.Workers.TransactionMonitorWorker
member.summary: Background service that monitors blockchain transactions for deployment status updates
member.remarks:
This worker polls blockchain networks at configurable intervals to check transaction confirmations and update deployment statuses accordingly. Supports both Algorand and EVM chains with automatic retry logic for transient failures.
NOTE: This is a placeholder implementation. The actual monitoring logic should be implemented based on the specific blockchain APIs and indexers available for each network. For production use, consider integrating with: - Algorand indexer APIs for transaction status - Block explorers APIs (Algoexplorer, Blockchair, Etherscan) - Chain-specific RPC endpoints with proper error handling
